prerequisites: A running RabbitMQ broker reachable at the host you will configure (set host; required).
The destination routing key that published messages will use (set routing_key; required).
A RabbitMQ user with permission to publish to the target virtual host. The built-in guest user can only connect over localhost, so create a dedicated user for remote connections.
If publishing through an exchange, an existing exchange and a binding between that exchange and the routing key (set exchange; optional).
Network access to the broker's AMQP port (default 5672, or 5671 when TLS is enabled).

authMethods.name: Username and password (AMQP PLAIN)
authMethods.summary: RabbitMQ authenticates clients with a username/password pair. Provide the broker username (username) and its password (password, secret). On a fresh broker the default user is guest/guest, but guest can only connect from localhost, so use a dedicated user for remote brokers. Both username and password are optional in the connector config since an anonymous or default-credential broker may not require them.

setupSteps.title: Create or identify a RabbitMQ user
setupSteps.body: On the broker, create a user (or reuse an existing one) and grant it access to the target virtual host with write permission so it can publish. The default guest user works only over localhost; create a dedicated user for any remote or production broker. Record the username and password for the connector's username and password fields.

setupSteps.title: Set the broker connection
setupSteps.body: Set host to the RabbitMQ server address. Optionally set port (defaults to 5672 for plain AMQP, or 5671 for AMQP over TLS). Set virtual_host if the broker uses a virtual host other than the default /; this scopes resources and permissions. Enable ssl to connect over TLS.

setupSteps.title: Configure the publishing target
setupSteps.body: Set routing_key (required) to the routing key that messages are published with. Optionally set exchange to publish through a named exchange; if you specify an exchange, make sure a binding exists between that exchange and the routing key. Leave exchange empty to publish to the default exchange using the routing key as the queue name.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials and save
setupSteps.body: Enter username and password if the broker requires authentication, then save and test the connection. The connector connects to host (and port), authenticates with the username/password over AMQP, and publishes records using the exchange and routing_key.
